metrics.name: Market solvency
metrics.desc:
The market is large and highly utilized, but borrower concentration is very low, with top five borrowers at only 2.78% of debt and top ten at 4.76%.
Historical bad debt is $0.00 despite 9,845 indexed liquidation events, which supports strong realized solvency performance.
The score is not 1 because the market has 0 reserves, 86.00% LLTV, high utilization, and 100.00% dependence on one BTC-correlated collateral asset.

metrics.name: Withdrawal liquidity
metrics.desc:
Immediate liquidity is large in dollar terms but only 11.25% of supplied assets.
Supplier concentration is high, with top five suppliers holding 91.44% and the largest supplier balance equal to 3.11x strict cash.
The score is not 4 because there is no identified persistent withdrawal shortage or market-specific withdrawal incident, and borrower concentration is very low.

metrics.desc:
cbBTC is listed, priced, and BTC-correlated, so it is materially stronger collateral than long-tail collateral.
Historical bad debt is $0.00, and stress evidence shows no bad-debt-zone debt under 10% or 20% collateral shocks.
The reduction is limited because the market is 100.00% dependent on one collateral asset, has 0 reserves, and uses an elevated 86.00% LLTV.

metrics.name: Collateral and liquidity diversification
metrics.desc:
Collateral diversification is weak because the market is backed by one asset only.
Borrower diversification is strong, with top five borrowers at only 2.78% and borrower HHI of 0.0003.
Supplier concentration is high, so the net diversification effect is mildly negative rather than neutral or strongly negative.

metrics.name: Market monitoring
metrics.desc:
The same assurance evidence is assumed to support live monitoring of market liquidity, utilization, borrowers, suppliers, liquidations, and bad debt.
Public observability reduces risk because market participants can identify liquidity and solvency deterioration.
The reduction is limited because internal alerting and response tooling are not verified.
